assertions.description: <usermedia> parses as a known HTML element backed by HTMLUserMediaElement.
assertions.test: (()=>{const e=document.createElement('usermedia');return typeof HTMLUserMediaElement !== 'undefined' && e instanceof HTMLUserMediaElement && !(e instanceof HTMLUnknownElement);})()

assertions.description: The HTMLUserMediaElement interface is exposed on window for the declarative capability element.
The <usermedia> element exposes a stream property for the acquired MediaStream.
The <usermedia> element exposes an error property for failed acquisition.
The <usermedia> element accepts media capture constraints through setConstraints().
The <usermedia> element exposes onstream for successful acquisition events.
The <usermedia> element exposes onerror and oncancel for failed or dismissed activation.
navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia remains available — capability elements supplement, not replace, the imperative API.
The 'camera' Permissions Policy feature continues to gate access — capability elements still respect Permissions Policy.
